# Capo

> Capo is a modern song library app for musicians. It organizes songs in [ChordPro](https://www.chordpro.org/) format with chords and lyrics, supports playlists, team collaboration, and public playlist sharing. Built with Next.js (App Router), Supabase, React Query, and TailwindCSS + shadcn/ui.

Capo uses a Feature-Based Architecture (FBA) where all business logic lives in `features/[feature-name]/`. Each feature exposes a public API through its `index.ts`. Cross-feature imports must always go through the public API (never from internal paths).

## Features

- [Song Library](/dashboard/songs): Browse and manage a personal song collection. Filter by musical key, BPM range, and status (active/archived). Songs are stored in ChordPro format with metadata including title, artist, key, BPM, capo, and transpose settings.
- [Lyrics & Chords Editor](/dashboard/songs/[id]): Split-pane interface with a CodeMirror-powered ChordPro editor and a live-rendered lyrics/chord view. Supports per-section chord colors, multi-column layout, and minimalist view mode.
- [Playlists](/dashboard/playlists): Create and manage playlists of songs. Songs can be reordered via drag-and-drop. Each playlist can be made public and shared via a unique share code.
- [Playlist Draft](/dashboard/playlists): A cart-like interface for quickly building playlists. Add songs from anywhere in the library into a draft, then save as a new or existing playlist.
- [Playlist Sharing](/shared/[shareCode]): Share any playlist publicly via a unique share code. Recipients can view the full playlist and individual song sheets without needing an account.
- [Song Creation & Editing](/dashboard/songs): Create new songs or edit existing ones using ChordPro format. The editor includes syntax highlighting, live preview, and all metadata fields.
- [Teams](/dashboard/teams): Create teams and invite other users by email. Team members share a song library and playlists. Switch between personal and team contexts.
- [Dashboard](/dashboard): Overview showing library stats (total songs, playlists, teams) and recently added songs.
- [Activity Tracking]: Tracks user engagement to surface relevant content.
- [Settings]: Switch between light and dark mode. Change the UI language (i18n support with validation).
- [Authentication]: Sign in with Google OAuth. Sessions are managed via Supabase Auth.

## App Routes

Public routes (no authentication required):
- `/` — Sign-in page
- `/shared/[shareCode]` — Publicly shared playlist view
- `/teams/accept-invitation` — Accept a team invitation

Protected routes (require authentication):
- `/dashboard` — Main dashboard
- `/dashboard/songs` — Song library browser
- `/dashboard/songs/[id]` — Individual song lyrics and chord view
- `/dashboard/playlists` — Playlist management
- `/dashboard/teams` — Team list
- `/dashboard/teams/new` — Create a new team
- `/dashboard/teams/[id]` — Team detail (members, invitations)
- `/dashboard/invitations` — Pending team invitations

## Tech Stack

- **Framework**: Next.js (App Router) with TypeScript 5
- **UI**: TailwindCSS 4 + shadcn/ui + Radix UI headless components
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Supabase Auth)
- **Server state**: React Query (@tanstack/react-query)
- **Client state**: React Context (scoped per feature)
- **Editor**: CodeMirror with ChordPro syntax highlighting
- **Song format**: ChordPro (parsed with chordsheetjs)
- **Forms**: react-hook-form + Zod validation
- **Drag and drop**: @dnd-kit and @hello-pangea/dnd
- **Testing**: Jest + @testing-library/react (80% coverage threshold)
- **i18n**: Custom hook with validated translation files

- [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/mfuentesg/capo-app/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): Developer commands, architecture rules, data layer patterns, and code style guidelines.
